acceptance: AUDOBS-0010 is resolved by explicitly scoping outer-ZIP byte reproducibility to one declared Python zipfile/zlib toolchain while retaining Git-tree and payload-manifest identity as the cross-toolchain invariant.
AUDOBS-0014 is resolved by creating a new remote without --push, verifying GitHub reports PRIVATE, and only then pushing commits and tags.
AUDOBS-0015 is resolved by treating Actions-permission lockdown failure as fatal after writing a failed receipt, and by surfacing label or issue setup failures as PARTIAL warnings.
AUDOBS-0016 is resolved by implementing typed command-scoped assurance gates that can BLOCK, WARN or remain ADVISORY, and invoking them from the operator CLI.
Unit tests directly exercise GitHub bootstrap ordering, failure policy, snapshot scope metadata and assurance-gate decisions.
The full Nexus Audit workflow passes on the pull-request head before promotion to main.
constraints: Do not modify baseline-001, canonical snapshots, R001/R002 target bytes, audit ledgers or preserved evidence.
Do not create a real external repository while testing github_bootstrap; use mocks only.
Do not claim universal ZIP-byte determinism.
Do not treat a passing test or CI run as complete security or correctness.
Preserve reports with status_authority NONE as evidence rather than silently converting them into authoritative dispositions.
objective: Close the R001 foundation audit cycle by implementing and directly testing the four remaining bounded remediations, then record a separate promotion receipt after CI succeeds.
